title: FindingNemo Extraction 1: Phenol-based Method
author: Inswasti Cahyani
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bxgnpjve
materials: Add 550 μl buffer-saturated phenol to each tube containing lysate.
Add 250 μl buffer-saturated phenol and 250 μl chloroform-isoamyl alcohol to each tube. Repeat step 11-12 and continue to step 15. go to step #11 go to step #12

steps.title: This protocol is a scaled-down and...
steps.instruction: This protocol is a scaled-down and modified version of the “ Ultra-long read sequencing protocol for RAD004 V.3 ” by Josh Quick ( https://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.mrxc57n ). Elution volume is adjusted for downstream application of preparing ultra-long DNA library following the new ONT protocol (SQK-ULK001).

steps.title: Pellet 5 million cells in a...
steps.instruction: Pellet 5 million cells in a 2 ml tube by centrifuging at 500 x g for 5 min at 4 o C. 500 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes

steps.title: Wash with 500 μl cold PBS...
steps.instruction: Wash with 500 μl cold PBS and centrifuge at 500 x g for 3 min at 4 o C. Discard supernatant. 500 x g, 4 °C, 3 minutes

steps.title: Resuspend well by pipette mixing in 20 μl cold PBS
steps.instruction: Resuspend well by pipette mixing in 20 μl cold PBS. Thorough resuspension is important for next lysis step.

steps.title: Add 1 ml TLB-SDS and 100...
steps.instruction: Add 1 ml TLB-SDS and 100 μg RNase A (1 μl) and vortex at full speed for 5 seconds. 2000 rpm, °C, 5 seconds

steps.title: Incubate at 37°C for 1 hour,...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37°C for 1 hour, mix by inversion every 15 minutes. 37 °C 01:00:00, mix per 15 min

steps.title: Add 200 μg Proteinase K (10 μl)
steps.instruction: Add 200 μg Proteinase K (10 μl). Mix by slow inversion 5 times.

steps.title: Incubate at 50°C for 2 hours,...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 50°C for 2 hours, mix every 30 minutes by slow inversion 3 times. 50 °C 02:00:00, mix per 30 min (3x inversion)

steps.title: Split the lysate into 2 phase-lock gel tubes (ca
steps.instruction: Split the lysate into 2 phase-lock gel tubes (ca. 550 ul per tube).

steps.title: Place on a HulaMixer or any...
steps.instruction: Place on a HulaMixer or any vertical rotator at 20 rpm for 10 minutes. If a fine emulsion has not formed after a minute gradually increase the rotation speed. 00:10:00, vertical rotator

steps.title: Centrifuge at 4500 rpm for 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 4500 rpm for 10 minutes. 4500 rpm, Room temperature, 10 minutes

steps.title: Transfer the aqueous phase to another...
steps.instruction: Transfer the aqueous phase to another phase-lock gel tube by pouring or using a wide-bore P1000 tip.

steps.title: Transfer and combine the aqueous phase...
steps.instruction: Transfer and combine the aqueous phase to a 2 ml tube (sample will be ca. 1 ml).

steps.title: Add 1 ml chloroform-isoamyl alcohol
steps.instruction: Add 1 ml chloroform-isoamyl alcohol. Repeat step 11-12 and continue to 17. go to step #11 go to step #12

steps.title: Using a wide-bore P1000 tip, transfer...
steps.instruction: Using a wide-bore P1000 tip, transfer the aqueous phase to a 5 ml conical tube. Do not bring any liquid from the interface of the chloroform phase, as this will affect DNA purity in the downstream steps.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.4x volume of 5M Ammonium Acetate (ca. 400 μl). Mix by slow inversion of tube.

steps.title: Add 3 clean glass beads
steps.instruction: Add 3 clean glass beads.

steps.title: Add 3 ml absolute ethanol
steps.instruction: Add 3 ml absolute ethanol.

steps.title: Rotate the tube with a vertical...
steps.instruction: Rotate the tube with a vertical rotator at 9 rpm for 5 minutes. 00:05:00, vertical rotator If a rotator is not available, hand inversion for 30-40 times can be used. Invert the tube slowly by hand so that a full inversion cycle takes 5-6 seconds.

steps.title: Remove solution, taking care not to...
steps.instruction: Remove solution, taking care not to disturb bound DNA on the glass beads.

steps.title: Wash bound DNA with 1 ml of 70% ethanol
steps.instruction: Wash bound DNA with 1 ml of 70% ethanol. Invert tube for 2-3 times and discard the ethanol.

steps.title: Repeat step 23
steps.instruction: Repeat step 23.

steps.title: Insert a bead retainer to a collection tube
steps.instruction: Insert a bead retainer to a collection tube. Pour the beads into the bead retainer and spin for 1 s in a mini centrifuge (or the shortest time possible) to remove residual wash buffer. Keep the bead retainer.

steps.title: Quickly pour the beads into a...
steps.instruction: Quickly pour the beads into a new 2 ml low-bind tube and immediately add 250 µl of elution buffer. Do not let the beads with DNA dry out. (As an alternative, 250 μl of elution buffer can be aliquoted into a 2 ml tube prior to this step.)

steps.title: Incubate at 37 o C for 30 min
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 o C for 30 min. Gently aspirate and dispense the eluate over the glass beads at regular intervals with a wide-bore P1000 tip to aid elution. 37 °C 00:30:00, mix per 10 min

steps.title: Insert the bead retainer from step...
steps.instruction: Insert the bead retainer from step 25 into a clean 2 ml DNA low-bind tube. Pour the beads from step 27 and centrifuge at 12,000 x g for 1 minute. 12000 rpm, Room temperature, 1 minute

steps.title: Add another 510 μl of elution...
steps.instruction: Add another 510 μl of elution buffer to the eluate from step 28 and mix with a wide-bore P1000 tip. Leave overnight at room temperature. Room temperature Overnight DNA sample will be viscous, but it is important to pipette thoroughly with a wide-bore pipette tip to ensure homogenization. Homogeneous DNA sample will ensure consistent output length and yield.

steps.title: Store at 4 o C or...
steps.instruction: Store at 4 o C or continue to UL Library Preparation as per Section " Modified ULK001 ". If only SQK-RAD004 is available, follow library preparation in Section " Modified RAD004 " or " KrazyStarFish (KSF) ". 4 °C, for storage

steps.title: Quantify DNA as per " UHMW...
steps.instruction: Quantify DNA as per " UHMW DNA QC " and check homogeneity by calculating %CV values. If the DNA is not sufficiently homegeneous, incubate the DNA for longer.
